title: oxalis articulata subsp. rubra
intro: Oxalis articulata subsp. rubra is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family, native to the Americas, characterized by its red flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation. The health benefits of Oxalis articulata subsp. rubra include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce cholesterol levels, and protect against certain diseases, such as cancer and diabetes. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
